title: Pit economics breakeven features
summary: F1 strategy references frame pit timing as a tradeoff between pit-lane loss and future time gained on fresher tyres; optimal stint length can be approximated from degradation and pit loss.
rationale: F1 strategy references frame pit timing as a tradeoff between pit-lane loss and future time gained on fresher tyres; optimal stint length can be approximated from degradation and pit loss. Current features include degradation ratios but not explicit breakeven economics.
implementation_hint: Estimate race-compound degradation slope from lap-time residuals versus tyre age after subtracting race-lap median pace. Add `estimated_optimal_stint = sqrt(2*pit_loss/slope)`, `tyrelife_minus_optimal`, `remaining_laps_gain_estimate`, and binned versions by Race/Compound/Stint. Use a fixed pit-loss proxy around 22-25 seconds or infer from abnormal pit laps if possible.
expected_effect: Gives tree models a compact, strategy-aligned signal for when tyres have crossed the value threshold to stop.
risk: Slope estimates can be noisy because lap time includes traffic, fuel burn, safety cars, and pit laps; robust clipping is needed.
